original_statement: "France and Spain are essentially co-favorites at around 17 percent [to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup according to prediction markets referenced in the PELE analysis]."
statement_context: Excerpt from comprehensive 100,000-simulation tournament forecast published by Silver Bulletin on 2026-06-03. Co-authored by Nate Silver and Joseph George using the PELE model.
resolution_criteria: On or around 2026-06-03, prediction markets implied approximately 17% probability for France to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, making them co-favorites with Spain, as referenced in Nate Silver's Silver Bulletin analysis.
source_text_snippet: In prediction markets, France and Spain are essentially co-favorites at around 17 percent, leaving plenty of win probability to divide among defending champion Argentina, England, Brazil, Portugal and others.
